reading_order.purpose: This staging model transforms raw e-commerce order data by renaming fields, converting cent values to dollars, and truncating timestamps to daily granularity for downstream analytics use.
reading_order.reason: architectural hub; significant business logic; data foundation

reading_order.purpose: This mart model aggregates order data with item-level details to create a comprehensive view of each order, including cost summaries, item counts, and customer ordering sequence. It enhances raw order data with business-relevant computed fields like whether an order contains food or drink items and the customer's order history rank.
reading_order.reason: architectural hub; core business logic; analytical output

reading_order.purpose: This model creates a comprehensive view of order items by joining staging tables for orders, products, and supplies to include product details, order timestamps, and supply costs. It aggregates supply costs per product and provides a unified dataset for downstream reporting and analysis.
reading_order.reason: architectural hub; core business logic; analytical output

reading_order.purpose: This staging model transforms raw e-commerce item data into a standardized format by renaming and selecting key fields. It prepares granular order item records for downstream analytics by mapping raw identifiers to business-friendly column names.
Provides a standardized way to convert monetary values from cents to dollars across different database platforms for consistent financial reporting.
reading_order.reason: core business logic; shared utility; entry point

reading_order.purpose: This file defines the data model and business metrics for order items, enabling analysis of revenue breakdown by food vs drink items, cost calculations, and profit metrics. It serves as a semantic layer for order item analytics.
reading_order.reason: core business logic; analytical output; entry point

reading_order.purpose: Maintains cleaned and validated order data with financial integrity checks, providing a reliable foundation for order-based business analysis and reporting.
reading_order.reason: core business logic; data foundation; entry point

reading_order.purpose: This file defines the 'orders' data mart, which provides a comprehensive view of order data including financial details, item types, and customer information. It serves as a centralized source for order-related analytics and reporting.
reading_order.reason: core business logic; analytical output; entry point

reading_order.purpose: Represents individual food and drink items that compose customer orders, enabling detailed order analysis and relationship tracking between order items and their parent orders.
reading_order.reason: core business logic; data foundation; entry point

reading_order.purpose: Generates a comprehensive date spine for time-series analysis and reporting across business metrics, though appears to be unused in current implementations.
reading_order.reason: significant business logic; analytical output; entry point

reading_order.purpose: This staging model transforms raw customer data from the ecom source by renaming fields to standardized column names. It selects only the customer ID and name, preparing the data for downstream usage in the analytics stack.

Creates a comprehensive customer analytics dataset that aggregates lifetime order history and spending patterns to classify customers as new or returning, enabling customer segmentation and retention analysis.
reading_order.reason: core business logic; analytical output; entry point

reading_order.purpose: This file defines a customer data mart that aggregates key customer-level metrics including lifetime spending, order counts, and customer type classification. It serves as a centralized source for customer analytics and supports downstream metrics like average order value and lifetime value calculations.
reading_order.reason: core business logic; analytical output; entry point

reading_order.purpose: Maintains cleaned and transformed customer data with one record per unique customer for consistent customer identification and management.
Defines and orchestrates the complete data workflow for the jaffle shop analytics project, including environment setup, data generation, and database loading processes.
Manages database schema organization by defining rules for where different types of data assets (seeds, models) should be deployed based on environment and naming conventions.
reading_order.reason: core business logic; shared utility; entry point

reading_order.purpose: Establishes the foundational data source definitions and metadata documentation for the e-commerce analytics system, mapping raw database tables to business entities.

This configuration file defines pre-commit hooks that enforce code quality standards and formatting rules before commits are accepted. It automates validation checks for YAML syntax, file formatting, and Python code style.
This dbt project configuration file defines the structure and settings for the 'jaffle_shop' analytics project, including model paths, deployment targets, and data transformation rules.

This staging model transforms raw product data from the e-commerce system into a standardized format suitable for analytics. It cleans and renames fields, converts price units, and adds boolean flags to categorize products as food or drink items.
reading_order.reason: architectural hub; core business logic; data foundation

reading_order.purpose: This staging model transforms raw supply data from the ecom source into a standardized format with cleaned column names, calculated fields, and appropriate data types for downstream analytics use.
Defines the supplies semantic model to enable business intelligence reporting on supply and product combinations, including supply characteristics like cost and perishability.
reading_order.reason: core business logic; analytical output; entry point

reading_order.purpose: Defines the product dimension for semantic modeling, providing standardized product attributes and categorizations to support consistent product-based metrics and analysis across the business.
reading_order.reason: significant business logic; analytical output; entry point

reading_order.purpose: Provides a cleaned and standardized view of product data for business reporting and analysis. Enables downstream consumers to access consistent product information without needing to understand the underlying raw data structure.
Delivers a unified view of supply chain data for operational reporting and inventory management decisions. Supports business users in understanding supply availability and relationships to products.
Manages cleaned and transformed product data representing food and drink items available for ordering, with one record per unique product.
Tracks supply expense data with basic cleaning and transformation, maintaining one row per supply cost (not per supply item). Multiple rows may exist per supply_id due to cost fluctuations over time.

This staging model transforms raw store data into a standardized format for downstream analytics use. It cleans and renames fields from the source while adding basic transformations like date truncation.

Establishes the location dimension for semantic modeling with standardized location attributes and a measure for average tax rate, enabling location-based business intelligence and financial analysis.
reading_order.reason: core business logic; analytical output; entry point

reading_order.purpose: Provides a simplified view of location data by selecting all records from the staging locations model without additional transformation.

Provides a cleaned and standardized list of open locations for business analysis, with each row representing a unique location and including key attributes like location ID, name, tax rate, and opening date.
